notes_md:
**mặc dù** = *although / even though*. It shows contrast between two ideas.

It’s a linking word (conjunction). You use it to connect two clauses that feel opposite or surprising.


The first clause is the “unexpected” situation, and the second clause is what still happens. Similar to **dù** (shorter, slightly more spoken) and close to **tuy … nhưng …** (a paired structure for contrast).

translation.en: I will go although it rains.

exampleSentencesTranslated: Although it was raining, he still went to work on time.
She tried very hard although the work was very difficult.
Although I am tired, I still want to go study Vietnamese.

definitions.en: A conjunction used to express contrast between two clauses, similar to 'although' or 'even though'.
definitions.vi: Từ dùng để chỉ sự trái ngược giữa hai mệnh đề, tương đương với 'dù' hoặc 'cho dù'.

example.en: Although it was raining, I still went to school on time.
example.vi: Mặc dù trời mưa, tôi vẫn đi học đúng giờ.

translation.en: I will go although it rains.

notes:
mặc dù = although / even though. It shows contrast between two ideas.

It’s a linking word (conjunction). You use it to connect two clauses that feel opposite or surprising.

The first clause is the “unexpected” situation, and the second clause is what still happens. Similar to dù (shorter, slightly more spoken) and close to tuy … nhưng … (a paired structure for contrast).
exampleSentences: Mặc dù trời mưa, anh vẫn đi làm đúng giờ.
Chị ấy đã cố gắng rất nhiều mặc dù công việc rất khó.
Mặc dù em mệt nhưng em vẫn muốn đi học tiếng Việt.
